notes: Read README.md, llm-tldr tree output, and sampled fafsa modules/tests.
No repo-local AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, CONTEXT.md, or docs/adr directory was present.
Pre-existing blocker: git status showed M .gitignore.

candidates.title: Split formula tables, calculations, and trace construction behind a Formula Engine Module
candidates.problem: The SAI Module combines input dataclasses, ED tables, helper formulas, dependent/independent calculations, citations, and trace construction. The Interface is broad, and each formula change requires reading the whole Implementation.
candidates.solution: Create a Formula Engine seam: immutable formula inputs in, structured trace out. Move ED tables and line calculations into named Modules that the engine composes.
candidates.benefits: Locality improves for ED-line defects, and Leverage improves because callers keep using prove_sai while the Implementation gains clearer formula seams.
candidates.test_impact: Tests can target line-level formula Modules plus end-to-end SAI traces.
candidates.risk: Moderate because the README reports the ED validation gate is currently red.

candidates.title: Deepen ISIR reconstruction as a validation adapter
candidates.problem: Fixed-width field extraction, missing-value handling, family reconstruction heuristics, and validation reporting live together. This makes the validation Adapter shallow and hard to debug when ED records fail.
candidates.solution: Separate ISIR parsing, family reconstruction, and comparison reporting Modules. Keep validate_isir_file as the public Adapter.
candidates.benefits: Locality improves for the red validation gate because parsing errors, reconstruction assumptions, and formula mismatches become distinguishable.
candidates.test_impact: Add focused tests for fixed-width parsing and reconstruction before SAI comparison.
candidates.risk: Low to moderate; the current red baseline must be preserved until correctness fixes are intentional.

candidates.title: Move fact intake and narration behind narrow adapters
candidates.problem: CLI, web app, wizard, and LLM backends each know details about family facts and trace presentation. That spreads user-facing behavior across several shallow Modules.
candidates.solution: Introduce a fact-intake Adapter Interface and a trace-presentation Adapter Interface. Keep the deterministic engine separate from extraction and narration.
candidates.benefits: Locality improves for UX changes and LLM backend changes. Leverage improves because all frontends can reuse the same fact schema and trace view.
candidates.test_impact: Backend tests can stay mocked while fact normalization and presentation get deterministic tests.
candidates.risk: Low; this can be introduced without changing engine math.
